## [03:04 - 05:54]

So in every one of those pre-launch videos, you know, the pre-launch content, every one of those videos, at the end of the video, you ask for them to make a comment. Or in between, you might be sending out an email, you ask for interaction in the email. You might have a PS, like the Magic PS, PS, I'd love to know what you're thinking. Just hit the reply button right now and let me know. And a lot of times you'll like, you'll say, hey, I'd love to know what you're thinking, but people just assume, you know, that's just sort of a rhetorical statement that you don't really mean it. But if you say PS, I would love to know what you think about this, period. Then say, so be period, hit your reply button right now and send me an email. Tell me what you think. Those are the magic words that you'll get a lot more interaction. It's not like everyone's going to respond to you or even, you know, 50%, but you will get a lot more interaction that way. So surveys are great for interaction. In your pre-launch content, every video at the end say, I'd love to know what you think. Let me know, you know, scroll down and tell me what you think in the comments. Or even better, instead of just what you think, give them a specific instruction. So like if you're teaching them, you know, how to play a song on the guitar, you can say, scroll down and tell me, like what was the first song you learned on the guitar? So ask them a very specific question. The more specific you are in your request, the more conversation, the more compliance, really. The more people are going to follow through and leave a comment or reply to you. Launch conversation is really, it's the magic lover that sort of lies inside product launch formula. A lot of people, until you go through a launch, you don't realize just how powerful this is. Because all of a sudden, as you're going through pre-launch and you're starting to get all these comments back, you can start to identify those objections. You can answer that in your next video. Or when it comes time to create that sales letter or that sales video, you are, what they call, Robert Collier wrote about this long ago. He's a famous copywriter. Robert Collier said, you want to enter the conversation in your prospect's mind. You want to enter the conversation that's already going on in their mind. Well, this, you know, the launch conversation is how you tap into that conversation. You tap into what's going on in their head. And then the same thing with your sales video. And your emails, each step of the way, you've entered the conversation in their mind. And that's why the launch conversation is so powerful.
